---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: b73e47f0d9df81be9e33d3f710ffe3f7a085bd47
findings:
  - { id: judged-ref-index-dc4-default-entries-vs-parent-dc5-enumeration, kind: judged, text: "ref-index dc-4 (with dc-3's Source=default) adds a third kind of index entry — default-branch specs enumerated by walking the default branch's tree — but parent dc-5 frames the directory enumeration as exactly two ref sources ('the index reads local design refs and remote-tracking design refs alike') and prescribes entry behavior only for those two kinds (a local design branch opens as an authoring wall; a remote-only branch renders sealed with its remoteness disclosed). No supersedes/exempts edge is declared for extending the enumeration beyond parent dc-5's stated sources, and the parent gives no open/sealed rule for a default-branch entry, leaving directory-home to invent one. Mitigating and confidence-lowering: dc-4's read is still refs-only and deterministic at a resolved ref (co-1 intact), each such entry is disclosed via Source=default (satisfying dc-5's disclosed-by-source rule), and parent dc-2's status groups ('active components', 'terminal') can only be populated from the ratified default-branch store — so dc-4 is plausibly implementing parent dc-2 rather than contradicting dc-5, but the extension of dc-5's source enumeration is nowhere declared as such. (confidence 0.40)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/ref-index#dc-1, spec/ref-index#dc-2, spec/ref-index#dc-3, spec/ref-index#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-1, spec/workbench-directory#dc-2, spec/workbench-directory#dc-3, spec/workbench-directory#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:e34ecc75969267546b9c1c3c7d6c721b0308846bc64c69361ec944615a3c67dc
integrity: sha256:7dbeeea38a3b6e7280527951c8a10ba95f0ae34ddd044e06bb0b8c061119c866
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"ref-index-dc4-default-entries-vs-parent-dc5-enumeration\",\"text\":\"ref-index dc-4 (with dc-3's Source=default) adds a third kind of index entry — default-branch specs enumerated by walking the default branch's tree — but parent dc-5 frames the directory enumeration as exactly two ref sources ('the index reads local design refs and remote-tracking design refs alike') and prescribes entry behavior only for those two kinds (a local design branch opens as an authoring wall; a remote-only branch renders sealed with its remoteness disclosed). No supersedes/exempts edge is declared for extending the enumeration beyond parent dc-5's stated sources, and the parent gives no open/sealed rule for a default-branch entry, leaving directory-home to invent one. Mitigating and confidence-lowering: dc-4's read is still refs-only and deterministic at a resolved ref (co-1 intact), each such entry is disclosed via Source=default (satisfying dc-5's disclosed-by-source rule), and parent dc-2's status groups ('active components', 'terminal') can only be populated from the ratified default-branch store — so dc-4 is plausibly implementing parent dc-2 rather than contradicting dc-5, but the extension of dc-5's source enumeration is nowhere declared as such.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-ref-index-dc4-default-entries-vs-parent-dc5-enumeration** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ref-index dc-4 (with dc-3's Source=default) adds a third kind of index entry — default-branch specs enumerated by walking the default branch's tree — but parent dc-5 frames the directory enumeration as exactly two ref sources ('the index reads local design refs and remote-tracking design refs alike') and prescribes entry behavior only for those two kinds (a local design branch opens as an authoring wall; a remote-only branch renders sealed with its remoteness disclosed). No supersedes/exempts edge is declared for extending the enumeration beyond parent dc-5's stated sources, and the parent gives no open/sealed rule for a default-branch entry, leaving directory-home to invent one. Mitigating and confidence-lowering: dc-4's read is still refs-only and deterministic at a resolved ref (co-1 intact), each such entry is disclosed via Source=default (satisfying dc-5's disclosed-by-source rule), and parent dc-2's status groups ('active components', 'terminal') can only be populated from the ratified default-branch store — so dc-4 is plausibly implementing parent dc-2 rather than contradicting dc-5, but the extension of dc-5's source enumeration is nowhere declared as such. (confidence 0.40)
